names.thesis: Mueller Industries is a copper-tube fabricator, not a metals price play, and posted record Q1 2026 results (net sales $1.19B, operating income +51.4%) on strong cost control and the Bison Metals copper-tube capacity acquisition. Northcoast downgraded to Neutral on May 28 after the stock's rally, saying risk-reward is now balanced, which caps near-term upside and supports the Aluminum-tagged SHORT bias in this book. Growth is real (reshoring, infrastructure, 40% dividend hike) but the stock has already re-rated and remains exposed to copper input-cost volatility and cyclical housing demand.
catalysts: Bison Metals Technologies integration boosting North American copper-tube capacity
Continued reshoring/infrastructure-driven volume demand
21-year dividend growth streak and near-zero leverage support capital returns
risks: Copper price volatility feeding raw material costs
Cyclical housing market exposure
Analyst risk-reward turned neutral post-rally (Northcoast downgrade)

evidence.note: Q1 2026 net sales $1.19B, operating income +51.4%, net income +51.8%; exposed to copper price volatility and cyclical housing
Northcoast downgrades MLI to Neutral from Buy citing balanced risk-reward after stock strength
CEO cites record Q1 earnings, Bison Metals acquisition boosting copper tube capacity, 40% dividend increase

names.thesis: POSCO Holdings' July CEO Investor Day laid out a KRW16.7 trillion (2026-2028) growth investment plan and a 'Triple-Core Portfolio' (Industrial Resources, Strategic Resources, Energy Resources), including expanding overseas steel capacity to 10Mtpa by 2031 (India, US, Indonesia) and optimizing listed-subsidiary stakes toward a 1.0x PBR target by 2028. This supports the Steel LONG thesis with added optionality from lithium/resource diversification and shareholder-friendly capital allocation (buybacks funded by stake sales).
catalysts: KRW16.7tn 2026-2028 growth capex and Triple-Core Portfolio execution
Overseas steel capacity expansion to 10Mtpa by 2031 in high-growth markets
Stake optimization + 10% of proceeds to buybacks targeting PBR re-rating to 1.0x by 2028
risks: China steel oversupply and export competition pressuring margins
Execution/capital-allocation risk diluting focus as lithium/energy pivot accelerates
Holding company discount could persist despite restructuring

evidence.note: CEO Investor Day: KRW16.7tn growth investment 2026-2028, stake optimization to ~50%, 10% of proceeds to buybacks
2028 targets: consolidated revenue KRW87.9tn, operating profit KRW6.7tn, overseas crude steel capacity to 10Mtpa by 2031
POSCO targets lithium/energy expansion alongside overseas steel capacity growth and shareholder return program

names.thesis: BHP's copper EBITDA contribution has more than doubled since FY23 to 51%, with production up ~30% over four years, and Berenberg raised its price target (GBP34 from GBP25) ahead of FY2026 results on Aug 18, expecting a bumper dividend. The company is actively reallocating capital toward core copper (weighing a $1.5-2B Chile desalination/energy asset sale) even as the Jansen potash project's cost rose to $6.9B with a ~$2.3B impairment and a two-year delay, illustrating the mixed non-copper portfolio drag typical of diversified miners.
catalysts: FY2026 results and fresh guidance due Aug 18, 2026
Potential $1.5-2B Chile desalination/energy asset sale to fund core copper growth
Antamina silver stream cash inflow ($4.3B) supporting balance sheet and dividend capacity
risks: Jansen potash cost overrun to $6.9B with $2.3B impairment and 2-year delay to FY2031 first production
Iron ore segment remains a drag amid China demand uncertainty
Net debt trending toward upper end of $10-20B target range

evidence.note: Copper EBITDA contribution >51% of total, more than doubled since FY23; copper production +~30% over 4 years
Berenberg raises BHP PT to GBP34, expects bumper dividend ahead of Aug 18 FY26 results
Jansen stage 2 potash cost raised to $6.9B from $4.9B, ~$2.3B impairment, first production delayed 2 years
BHP evaluating $1.5-2B sale of Chile desalination/energy assets to fund core copper business

names.thesis: Rio Tinto is reducing its legacy iron-ore over-dependence and benefiting from resilient iron ore, copper and aluminium prices, with a $5-10B portfolio simplification program underway (TiO2/Borates sale, $650M opex savings already booked) and Oyu Tolgoi copper shipments restored after a brief Mongolia blockade. However, the picture is genuinely mixed for this diversified miner: Berenberg expects H1 earnings to miss consensus on a revised commodity deck, and RBC downgraded to Underperform, arguing upside is hard to see outside aluminium (28% of EBITDA) with iron ore pricing already assuming $102/t and vulnerable to Chinese economic softness.
catalysts: Portfolio simplification targeting $5-10B unlocked value, $650M opex savings already achieved
TiO2/Borates business sale process underway plus potential by-product streaming
Oyu Tolgoi copper shipments normalized after Mongolia road blockade
risks: Berenberg models H1 revenue/EPS below consensus ($30.2B vs $30.8B; EPS $3.87 vs $4.19)
RBC downgrade to Underperform citing limited upside ex-aluminium and stretched valuation (5.5x on spot)
Iron ore pricing already assumes $102/t; Chinese demand softness is a key swing risk

evidence.note: AlphaValue/Baader raises PT, notes Rio reducing iron ore over-dependence, right commodity mix, EPS forecast raised 38.5% for 2026
Berenberg expects H1 earnings miss on revised commodity deck; notes resilient iron ore/copper/aluminium prices YTD
RBC downgrades to Underperform, sees limited upside outside aluminium, iron ore priced at $102/t, prefers Glencore
Oyu Tolgoi copper concentrate shipments resumed after brief blockade interruption

names.thesis: Eaton is a demand-side beneficiary of electrification and AI data-center capex rather than a direct metals-price trade: Q1 2026 adjusted EPS hit a first-quarter record ($2.81), data-center orders in Electrical Americas jumped 240% YoY, and full-year guidance was raised to $13.50 from $13.05. The pending Mobility Group/Dana combination (expected ~$1.1B cash distribution, accretive to margins) sharpens the portfolio toward Electrical/Aerospace megatrends, but valuation already reflects much of this AI-driven momentum, leaving little room for disappointment.
catalysts: Data center order growth +240% YoY in Electrical Americas, backlog +48% YoY
FY2026 adjusted EPS guidance raised to $13.50 from $13.05
Mobility Group/Dana combination expected accretive with ~$1.1B cash distribution
risks: Valuation already prices in strong AI/electrification momentum
Portfolio separation (Mobility Group spin) execution risk
Limited direct leverage to steel/electrical-steel price swings versus pure metals names

evidence.note: Q1 2026 record adjusted EPS $2.81; Electrical Americas orders +240% YoY; FY26 guidance raised to $13.50
Mobility Group/Dana combination expected accretive to organic growth and margins, ~$1.1B cash distribution
Eaton framed as hidden AI/grid-modernization winner but valuation leaves little room for disappointment

names.thesis: Gold Fields delivered a strong operational quarter (Q1 2026 production +15% YoY, guidance reaffirmed) with an 8% five-year production CAGR pipeline and trades at a valuation discount to peers (5.0x EV/EBITDA vs. 6x peer average), and analysts including Canaccord (upgraded to Buy) and JPMorgan (Overweight) stayed constructive even as gold pulled back from highs. Despite genuinely strong fundamentals, the book's short-medium gold macro view caps enthusiasm: the name is a pure-play on a metal we expect to cool from the current $4,100+ tape.
catalysts: 8% five-year production CAGR with Windfall (Tier-1) project nearing construction
Q1 2026 production +15% YoY, full-year guidance reaffirmed
Valuation discount to gold-miner peer group on EV/EBITDA and FCF yield
risks: Gold price backdrop rated short-medium despite current $4,100+ spot strength
Single-commodity concentration to a gold price reversal
African/South African operating jurisdiction and cost inflation risk

evidence.note: Q1 production +15% YoY, guidance reaffirmed; Canaccord upgraded to Buy, JPMorgan Overweight
BofA Global Metals conference deck: 8% five-year production CAGR, Windfall project nearing execution, industry-leading FCF yield

names.thesis: Zijin Gold International (parent Zijin Mining, the world's top gold miner) is expanding output via Kolwezi/Musoshi copper mine ramp-up and a new Norton Gold Fields (Australia) crushing system adding ~11,600 oz/year of gold, and just debuted at #1,111 on the Forbes Global 2000. Operationally it is executing well and diversifying into copper/lithium, but as a primarily gold-exposed name it sits within the book's short-medium gold view, and as a US OTC ADR (ZIJMF) it carries meaningful IBKR tradability/liquidity risk versus the HK-listed shares.
catalysts: Norton Gold Fields Australia new crushing system lifting annual gold output ~11,600 oz
Kolwezi/Musoshi copper mine ramp-up diversifying revenue beyond gold
Parent Zijin Mining's rising global scale (Forbes Global 2000 #181, up 70 places)
risks: Gold price backdrop rated short-medium by the book
OTC ADR (ZIJMF) liquidity and IBKR tradability risk versus HK primary listing
DRC/African jurisdiction concentration risk at Kolwezi/Musoshi

evidence.note: Zijin ranked #181 on Forbes Global 2000, up 70 places; Zijin Gold International debuted at #1,111; world's top gold miner
Norton Gold Fields Australia commissions new crushing system ahead of schedule, +11,600 oz/yr gold output
Zijin Gold International's Kolwezi/Musoshi copper mines and Julong copper mine production steadily increasing

names.thesis: Euro Manganese's Chvaletice project PEA confirms a base-case post-tax NPV of $492M (13.8% IRR) at an HPMSM price of $2,888/t, with upside to $798M NPV if prices reach $3,275/t, but the company just amended its Orion facility to convert debt into a royalty (2.29-2.46% of project revenue) to shore up its balance sheet ahead of financing conditions that still require securing offtakes for 40% of first-five-year production and a strategic investor. This is a pre-revenue optionality name whose near-term catalysts are financing milestones rather than manganese price moves, consistent with a neutral manganese macro stance.
catalysts: Chvaletice PEA base case NPV $492M / IRR 13.8% (upside case $798M at higher HPMSM prices)
Orion facility amendment converts debt to royalty, easing near-term balance sheet pressure
Potential offtake agreements (40% of first 5-year production) and strategic investor could unlock project financing
risks: Pre-production, pre-revenue stage; project financing still contingent on offtakes and a strategic investor
Cumulative royalty stack (up to ~4.75% combined across facilities) dilutes long-run project economics
Manganese price backdrop rated neutral, limited near-term commodity catalyst

evidence.note: Euro Manganese amends Orion facility, converts outstanding loan to a 2.29-2.46% revenue royalty pending fundraise condition
Chvaletice PEA base case: post-tax NPV $492M, IRR 13.8% at HPMSM $2,888/t; upside case NPV $798M at $3,275/t
Key financing condition: offtake agreements for 40% of first 5 years' production plus a strategic investor required for $30M tranche drawdown

names.thesis: Jinduicheng Molybdenum is riding a sharp molybdenum price rally (oxide/ferromolybdenum up >30% YTD 2026, Shanghai spot nearly doubling from RMB 464/t in January to RMB 591/t in June), which drove Q1 2026 revenue up 26.7% YoY and net profit up 33.0% YoY. However, the stock has already more than doubled YTD and its price-to-book ratio just hit a decade high after a string of limit-up days before pulling back, suggesting much of the 'computing power metal' rally is priced in; as a Shanghai A-share it also carries meaningful IBKR/foreign-investor accessibility constraints (Stock Connect/QFII).
catalysts: Molybdenum oxide/ferromolybdenum prices up >30% YTD 2026, direct earnings pass-through
Q1 2026 revenue +26.7% YoY, net profit +33.0% YoY
Broader China 'computing power metal' sector rally sentiment (minor metals/rare earths)
risks: Stock +100%+ YTD with PB at a decade high; valuation has run ahead of fundamentals
China A-share listing limits IBKR/foreign investor access (Stock Connect/QFII required)
Molybdenum backdrop rated neutral by the book - rally may be overextended

evidence.note: Moly oxide/ferromolybdenum prices +30%+ YTD 2026; stock PB at decade high after limit-up run, then pullback
Shanghai moly spot price nearly doubled from RMB464/t (Jan 5) to RMB591/t (Jun 11); Q1 2026 revenue +26.67% YoY, net profit +32.99% YoY

names.thesis: Aperam guided Q2 2026 adjusted EBITDA 'significantly higher' than Q1's EUR90m (consensus EUR121m), helped by EU trade defense measures taking effect July 1 (reducing import competition and lifting European mill utilization) and Brazil's return to full seasonal strength, plus continued strength in Alloys & Specialities (LNG, aerospace, electrical). But management has flagged 'low' European stainless demand for three consecutive quarters with no change, and the sequential improvement is being driven by trade barriers and seasonality rather than a genuine nickel/stainless price recovery - consistent with the book's neutral-to-bearish nickel/stainless stance.
catalysts: EU trade defense measures effective July 1, 2026 lifting European mill capacity utilization
Brazil returning to full seasonal strength in Q2 2026
Alloys & Specialities order book strength in LNG, electrical/electronics, aerospace
risks: European stainless demand still described as 'low'/challenging for 3 straight quarters
Improvement driven by trade protection/seasonality, not underlying nickel/stainless price recovery
Technical picture shows bearish short-term moving average signals

evidence.note: Q2 2026 adjusted EBITDA guided significantly higher than Q1's EUR90m; consensus EUR121m; trade defense and Brazil seasonality cited as drivers
Aperam confirms 'significant' sequential EBITDA increase in Q2 2026, driven by trade defense and Brazil seasonal return
Technical analysis shows neutral-to-bearish trend with bearish short-term moving average signals

names.thesis: Morgan Stanley cut Vale on July 8, citing weaker iron ore prices from expanding global supply surpluses, softer Chinese steel production, and rising production costs, now expecting 2026/27 earnings below consensus - despite 'continued progress' in the base metals (copper) business, which the same note flags as MS's preferred sector-wide commodity. Vale's nickel segment remains highly price-sensitive (EBITDA could range from ~$1.15B to ~$2.0B in 2026 depending on whether nickel trades $16k-$20k/t), consistent with this book's neutral-to-bearish nickel stance. Net: iron ore drag currently dominates the diversified-miner copper tailwind for Vale specifically, ahead of Q2 2026 production/sales (Jul 21) and earnings (Jul 30) reports.
catalysts: Q2 2026 production/sales report July 21, financial results July 30, 2026
Continued progress in base metals/copper business per Morgan Stanley
Nickel segment has meaningful upside leverage if prices rally toward $20k/t
risks: Morgan Stanley downgrade/estimate cut on weaker iron ore prices and supply surplus
Softer Chinese steel production and rising production costs pressuring 2026/27 EPS below consensus
Iron ore remains Vale's dominant revenue driver despite copper/nickel diversification

analyst.note: Prefers copper sector-wide; cautious on iron ore and aluminum

evidence.note: Morgan Stanley downgrades Vale on weaker iron ore prices, supply surplus, softer Chinese steel, rising costs; 2026/27 EPS below consensus
Nickel segment EBITDA sensitivity: ~$1,150-$2,000M in 2026 depending on nickel price ($16k-$20k/t)
Q2 2026 production/sales report Jul 21; financial results Jul 30; conference call Jul 31

names.thesis: Defense Metals commenced a five-week pilot flotation program at SGS Lakefield in May 2026 to de-risk the Wicheeda rare earth project's process flowsheet, and separately signed a non-binding MOU with South Korea's Hanwha Ocean to explore a rare earth offtake agreement and potential strategic investment - both consistent with the book's LONG rare earths thesis amid the sharp NdPr price rally. As a pre-revenue TSXV developer, the name is high-risk/high-optionality: the Hanwha MOU is non-binding and no financing or firm offtake is yet secured.
catalysts: Pilot flotation program results due to de-risk Wicheeda process flowsheet and support future engineering studies
Potential conversion of Hanwha Ocean MOU into a binding offtake agreement and/or strategic investment
Strong NdPr/rare earth pricing macro (+74% per book) supporting project economics
risks: Pre-revenue, pre-construction developer with no offtake or project financing secured
TSXV micro-cap liquidity constraints and IBKR tradability risk
Hanwha MOU is non-binding; conversion to firm commercial terms uncertain

evidence.note: Defense Metals begins pilot flotation program at SGS Lakefield for Wicheeda rare earth project, ~5 weeks, using 30 tonnes of drill core
Defense Metals signs non-binding MOU with Hanwha Ocean for rare earth offtake collaboration and potential strategic investment

names.thesis: Hochschild Mining delivered steady Q1 2026 output (1,912koz silver, 64koz gold) and maintained full-year guidance (300-328koz gold-equivalent oz), and peer commentary frames the broader silver/gold macro as structurally bullish in 2026. Despite that, Berenberg cut its price target to 570p from 590p (Hold) and JPMorgan also trimmed its target by 40p (while keeping Overweight), with shares falling 4.2% on the Berenberg cut - name-specific target resets are running counter to the sector narrative, supporting a short-low stance alongside the book's broader silver caution.
catalysts: Q1 2026 production of 1,912koz silver and 64koz gold, full-year guidance maintained
Structurally bullish silver/gold macro cited across sector comparables
AGM resolutions passed cleanly, including Pelham Concert Party stake waiver
risks: Berenberg cut PT to 570p from 590p (Hold), shares fell 4.2% on the news
JPMorgan also reduced its target ~40p despite maintaining Overweight
Elevated cost guidance ($2,157-2,320 per AuEq oz)

evidence.note: Q1 2026 production 1,912koz silver, 64koz gold; FY26 guidance maintained at 300-328koz AuEq; JPMorgan cut target 40p in April despite Overweight
Hochschild shares fell 4.2% after Berenberg cut price target to 570p from 590p, maintained Hold

names.thesis: Korea Zinc's core zinc/lead/tin franchise sits within the book's LONG zinc/tin thesis, and the company has been building critical-minerals optionality via its strategic partnership and $85.2M equity investment in TMC (deep-sea nodule processing/pCAM refining technology). The bigger near-term overhang is governance: the prolonged MBK Partners/Young Poong hostile takeover battle continues, with shares falling 4% in late June amid labor union pushback, and as a Korea Exchange-listed name it carries real IBKR tradability/access constraints for foreign investors.
catalysts: Zinc/lead/tin backdrop rated LONG by the book
Strategic partnership with TMC for pCAM/non-ferrous refining technology, diversifying into battery materials
Potential resolution of MBK control battle could remove an overhang on the stock
risks: Prolonged MBK Partners hostile takeover/control battle; shares fell 4% on renewed tension (2026-06-30)
Korea Exchange listing - IBKR tradability/foreign access risk
Labor union opposition adds governance and execution uncertainty

evidence.note: Korea Zinc shares fell 4% amid renewed MBK Partners takeover battle; labor union opposition to MBK control
Korea Zinc holds $85.2M equity stake plus warrants in TMC, partnering on non-ferrous refining/pCAM technology for nickel-copper-cobalt processing

names.thesis: Nexa Resources posted a strong Q1 2026 (adjusted EBITDA +126% YoY to $283M, revenue +42% YoY to $888M, zinc production +18% YoY with a record Aripuanã quarter), and despite a May 13 Cajamarquilla smelter fire that will cost ~7,000 tonnes (2% of annual output) of refined zinc, the company maintained full-year 2026 zinc production guidance (+6% at the midpoint) with recovery expected in H2. Management also flagged a constructive zinc market structure - low exchange inventories, easing mine supply, recovering treatment charges - reinforcing the book's LONG zinc stance, though the stock's 164% one-year rally means much of the good news may already be reflected in price.
catalysts: 2026 zinc production guidance maintained (+6% YoY at midpoint) despite Cajamarquilla disruption
Record Aripuanã mine output driving 18% YoY zinc production growth
Favorable zinc market structure: low exchange inventories, easing mine supply, recovering treatment charges
risks: Cajamarquilla smelter fire cut ~7,000t refined zinc (2% of annual output), weighing on Q2
Execution risk on H2 2026 production recovery at Cajamarquilla
Stock +164% over the past year; forward P/E of 5.02x is cheap vs. industry 13.66x but sentiment already very positive

evidence.note: Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA +126% YoY to $283M; zinc production +18% YoY; revenue +42% YoY to $888M; record Aripuanã output
Cajamarquilla fire cuts ~7,000t refined zinc (2% of annual output); FY26 zinc guidance maintained, +6% YoY at midpoint; forward P/E 5.02x vs industry 13.66x
Zinc market to benefit from easing mine supply, recovering treatment charges, low exchange inventories; copper market structurally tight too
